mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever FEDFUNDS remains above its trailing 12-month average.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive median 60d forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the effect disappears (t-stat < 1.5) once controlling for concurrent PAYEMS trend.

mechanism_narrative: S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days are driven positively only when retail sales growth (RSAFS) and industrial production growth (INDPRO) are simultaneously accelerating, since joint demand-and-supply expansion confirms a broad-based growth signal that neither series alone reliably captures.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0 AND INDPRO MoM growth > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-acceleration episodes fail to outperform single-series acceleration episodes by a statistically significant margin (bootstrap p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated, further increases in FEDFUNDS forecast negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as tightening compounds credit-cost drag on equity valuations, but this relationship reverses or disappears outside tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising for 2+ consecutive months
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising, 60d forward returns are not significantly more negative than the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05), or the sign flips in more than one out of three tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving corporate output and near-term earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week (5 trading days) as markets incorporate the surprise before the next data release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM change and forward 5d return is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no exploitable short-horizon signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% over 3 months signals weakening consumer demand and construction-sector stress that spreads to broader equity sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline episodes show forward 20d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical decades.

mechanism_narrative: S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter turn negative specifically when payroll growth (PAYEMS) decelerates while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, because the combination of weakening labor demand and restrictive policy jointly signals a coming profit squeeze that neither series predicts alone.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes underperform single-condition (PAYEMS-only or FEDFUNDS-only) episodes by less than a statistically significant margin (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained economic expansion identified by positive real GDP growth (GDPC1), month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) forecasts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as consumer strength confirms continued corporate revenue momentum; this relationship weakens or inverts outside expansion regimes.
regime_hint: GDP expansion regime (GDPC1 QoQ growth positive)
failure_condition: During confirmed GDP expansion quarters, RSAFS acceleration fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 with forward 5d returns over rolling 2-year windows, or the sign of the relationship flips in more than one expansion episode.
